Client 2: And I want it to feel doable month to month. If the plan looks great on paper but cash flow is tight, it won’t stick.
Advisor: That makes sense. Let’s anchor on the basics first—income and spending—then we’ll map the accounts and debts. Davi, what’s the household gross income per year right now, all-in?
Client 1: Household gross is 300900 annually.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. Susan, does that include your self-employed income as well, or is there anything separate like bonuses that hit monthly?
Client 2: It includes it. I do have a monthly bonus line that rolls up into that—so yeah, it’s all part of the 300900.

Client 2: It includes it. I do have a monthly bonus line that rolls up into that—so yeah, it’s all part of the 300900.
Advisor: Perfect. Now on the outflow side—about what are your total monthly expenses, just to run the household and life as it is today?
Client 2: All-in, it’s 21950 a month. It’s… a lot, but with three kids it adds up fast.
Advisor: Yeah, I hear you. One sec—just noting that down. Within that, how much is going to debt payments each month in total—mortgage plus any other loans?
Client 2: I think it’s like seventy-one hundred? Wait—yeah, total monthly debt payments is 7100.

Client 2: All-in, it’s 21950 a month. It’s… a lot, but with three kids it adds up fast.
Advisor: Yeah, I hear you. One sec—just noting that down. Within that, how much is going to debt payments each month in total—mortgage plus any other loans?
Client 2: I think it’s like seventy-one hundred? Wait—yeah, total monthly debt payments is 7100.
Advisor: Okay. And just so I’m not mixing pieces—what makes up that 7100? Is most of it the mortgage?
Client 1: Yes. The mortgage payment is 6850 a month, and then there’s a smaller loan payment of 200 a month.

Client 1: Yes. The mortgage payment is 6850 a month, and then there’s a smaller loan payment of 200 a month.
Advisor: Thanks. While we’re on the big picture, let’s talk investable assets—so not the house value, but the accounts you can invest from. Roughly what’s the total across everything?
Client 1: Total investable assets is 975800.
Advisor: Great. We’ll break that into buckets so we can talk risk and taxes. Davi, what’s the big taxable account sitting at right now—the one at the bank?
Client 1: That taxable account is 544800.

Client 1: Yes, private markets on an advisor platform—84,200. Sorry—84,200.

Client 2: It’s ugly—24.42%.

Advisor: Makes sense. Switching gears—taxes. Do you know roughly what bracket you’re landing in these days? Not the exact effective rate, just the bracket band.
Client 2: I think we’re in 32%.
Advisor: Okay—32%. Thanks. Last admin-ish thing: how are you engaging me—are we doing this through an RIA, like a registered investment advisor relationship, or something else?
Client 1: It’s RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Perfect. And just to close the loop on protection—you both said there’s nothing active you’re counting on right now for life insurance or disability outside work, correct?

Advisor: Hi Davi, hi Susan—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into accounts, what would you say you want this plan to do for your family over the next few years?
Client 1: Thanks. I want a clear retirement path and to make sure we’re not missing anything for the kids—like, guardrails, not just “invest aggressively and hope.”
Client 2: And I want it to feel doable month to month. If the plan looks great on paper but cash flow is tight, it won’t stick.
Advisor: That makes sense. Let’s anchor on the basics first—income and spending—then we’ll map the accounts and debts. Davi, what’s the household gross income per year right now, all-in?

Client 2: I’m okay with aggressive too, as long as we keep enough cash so the monthly stuff doesn’t get scary.
Advisor: Makes sense. Switching gears—taxes. Do you know roughly what bracket you’re landing in these days? Not the exact effective rate, just the bracket band.
Client 2: I think we’re in 32%.
Advisor: Okay—32%. Thanks. Last admin-ish thing: how are you engaging me—are we doing this through an RIA, like a registered investment advisor relationship, or something else?
Client 1: It’s RIA (registered investment advisor).
